Hero.description: We defend life, strengthen identity, and pave ways of dignity, memory, and future for the Afro communities of Putumayo.

Featured.title: Librada Maturana; the legacy of a Maroon woman who fought for equality, equity, and territory.
Featured.excerpt: To speak of Librada Maturana is to speak of living memory, resistance, dignity, and deep love for the Afro people of Putumayo. Her legacy is not only measured in years of struggle but in the seeds left in her community's conscience...

Infographic.title: Maroonage and Resistance
Infographic.subtitle: From Palenque to Putumayo. A timeline of Afro presence in the region.

1.title: Ruby Maturana, heiress of a story that continues to transform lives today
1.excerpt: Ruby Maturana embodies the strength of a legacy that did not stop, but flourished in a new generation. As the daughter of Librada Maturana, today she leads with love...
1.author: The New Leadership

2.title: The Putumayo jungle is our sustenance
2.excerpt: For the Afro-Maroon communities of Putumayo, the jungle is not a distant landscape or a simple green background. It is home, food, medicine...
2.author: Territory

3.title: Uramba Cimarrón: The collective work that sustains memory
3.excerpt: In Cimarrón Putumayo, the word Uramba does not only name a community activity. It names a way of living, resisting, and building together.
3.author: Community

Terms.title: Terms and Conditions of Use
Terms.subtitle: Departmental Association for Human Rights of Community Councils, Grassroots Organizations and other forms Cimarrón Putumayo

sections.title: 1. Acceptance of Terms
sections.content: By accessing and using the Cimarrón Putumayo website (hereinafter, 'the Site'), you agree to be bound by these Terms and Conditions. If you disagree with any part of these terms, you must not use our website.

sections.title: 2. Intellectual Property
sections.content: All content present on the Site, including but not limited to texts, photographs, graphics, logos, icons, audio, videos, and software, is the exclusive property of Cimarrón Putumayo or its respective community authors, and is protected by Colombian intellectual property laws and international treaties. Its reproduction, distribution, or modification without prior written authorization is strictly prohibited.

sections.title: 3. Acceptable Use of the Site
sections.content: You agree to use the Site only for lawful purposes and in a way that does not infringe the rights of, restrict or inhibit anyone else's use and enjoyment of the Site. The use of the site to send unsolicited mail (spam), transmit viruses, or publish offensive or defamatory content is prohibited.

sections.title: 4. Donations Policy
sections.content: Donations made through our Site are voluntary contributions intended to support the mission and projects of Cimarrón Putumayo. Once a donation is processed, refunds will not be issued, except in exceptional cases of a verifiable technical error in the payment platform. By donating, you declare that the funds come from lawful sources.

sections.title: 5. Third-Party Links
sections.content: The Site may contain links to third-party websites. Cimarrón Putumayo has no control over the content, privacy policies, or practices of such websites, and assumes no responsibility for them.

sections.title: 6. Modifications
sections.content: Cimarrón Putumayo reserves the right to modify these Terms and Conditions at any time. Changes will take effect immediately upon posting on the Site. Your continued use of the Site after any changes constitutes your acceptance of the new Terms.

sections.title: 7. Applicable Law
sections.content: These Terms and Conditions are governed by the laws of the Republic of Colombia. Any dispute related to the use of this Site will be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts in Colombia.

Privacy.subtitle1: Departmental Association for Human Rights of Community Councils, Grassroots Organizations and other forms

sections.title: 1. General Overview
sections.content: The Departmental Association for Human Rights of Community Councils, Grassroots Organizations and other forms CIMARRÓN PUTUMAYO, identified with NIT 846003787-0... adopts this Policy to guarantee the protection, confidentiality, and adequate processing of the personal data of its associates, beneficiaries, project participants, employees, contractors, suppliers, allies, and other data owners.

sections.title: 2. Data Controller
list: Entity: Asociación Departamental por los Derechos Humanos de Consejos Comunitarios, Organizaciones de Base y demás formas CIMARRÓN PUTUMAYO
NIT: 846003787-0
Legal Representative: CORTES CORTES MARLENY
Address: Carrera 21 # 31-90, Barrio El Jardín, Puerto Asís - Putumayo
Email: putumayocimarron@gmail.com
Contact Phone: 3113590790

sections.title: 3. Data Collected
sections.content: The entity collects and stores personal information necessary for its operational and administrative activities, such as:
list: First and last names
ID type and number
Contact info: address, municipality, phone, email
Photographs and audiovisual material in activities
Academic, employment, or social participation information
Banking or financial data for contractual payments
Sensitive data (ethnic origin, community, health, biometric information), only when strictly required for specific projects and with express authorization.

sections.title: 4. Processing Purposes
sections.content: Personal data will be used for:
list: Registration of associates, representatives, members, volunteers, and beneficiaries.
Execution of social, humanitarian, human rights, environmental, cultural, academic, or community projects.
Contact for open calls, workshops, events, campaigns, and training processes.
Management of contractors, suppliers, and allies.
Security, attendance control, and evidence of activities.
Reports to public, private, funding, and international cooperation entities.
Accounting, labor, tax, and contractual management.
Sending institutional, educational information, and official communications.
sections.extraContent: Information will not be sold or delivered to third parties without authorization except for legal obligations.

sections.title: 5. Rights of the Data Subject
list: Consult their data.
Request updates, corrections, or deletion.
Revoke authorization.
File complaints with the Superintendency of Industry and Commerce.

sections.title: 6. Handling Requests
sections.content: The data subject may exercise their rights through:
list: Institutional email: putumayocimarron@gmail.com
Address: Carrera 21 # 31-90, Barrio El Jardín, Puerto Asís – Putumayo
sections.extraContent:
Legal response times:
Inquiries: up to 10 business days
Claims: up to 15 business days

sections.title: 7. Data of Minors
sections.content: The Association will only process data of minors when linked to social, educational, or human rights projects, protected by the best interests of the minor and with the authorization of the legal representative.

sections.title: 8. Security and Confidentiality
sections.content: The Association will implement technical, human, and administrative measures to:
list: Protect information.
Prevent loss, unauthorized access, alteration, or misuse.
Ensure safe preservation of physical and digital files.

sections.title: 9. Data Transfer
list: Public entities (ICBF, mayoralties, governorships, ministries).
Allied or funding organizations for project execution.
Control entities when required by law.
sections.extraContent: Always under the duty of confidentiality and protection.

sections.title: 10. Validity
sections.content: This Policy is effective from its publication, and the data will be stored as long as:
list: A relationship with the data subject is maintained.
Legal or contractual obligations exist.
Deletion is not requested within the limits of the law.

AboutSection.title:
We are roots.
We are memory.
We are Putumayo.
AboutSection.p: The Cimarrón Putumayo Association is born from collective work, the defense of rights, Afro-descendant culture, and love for the territory. Our work connects generations, protects ancestral knowledge, and strengthens community processes that build dignity.

1.title: Culture and Identity
1.text: We rescue the music, dance, gastronomy, hairstyles, oral tradition, and ancestral knowledge of the Afro-Putumayo people.

2.title: Territory and Nature
2.text: We recognize the jungle, the river, and the land as sustenance, memory, medicine, and a school of life for our communities.

3.title: Rights and Community
3.text: We promote leadership, participation, opportunities for women and youth, economic autonomy, and the defense of dignity.

1.title: URAMBA CIMARRÓN
1.text: Collective work, solidarity, and community organization.

2.title: AFROPUTUMAYO ETHNODEVELOPMENT
2.text: Processes to strengthen autonomy, identity, and territorial development.

3.title: INNOVATIVE AFRO TALENT
3.text: Training, leadership, and opportunities for young Afro-Putumayans.

4.title: GUARDIANS OF THE AMAZONIAN RIVERS
4.text: Care of the territory, nature, and environmental knowledge.

5.title: TRAINING IN RIGHTS AND LEADERSHIP
5.text: Pedagogical processes to strengthen citizenship, equity, and participation.

CasaAfroSection.subtitle: Our Headquarters
CasaAfroSection.title:
Librada Maturana
Afro-Putumayan House
CasaAfroSection.p1: Located in Puerto Asís, the Afro House is the heart of our association. A space for meeting, resistance, culture, and brotherhood for all Black communities in Putumayo.
CasaAfroSection.p2: Here we hold workshops, musical rehearsals, training, and assemblies. It is our house, where roots grow strong and identity is celebrated every day.

FeaturedStorySection.title: Women weavers of memory on the Putumayo River
FeaturedStorySection.p: Through weaving and traditional medicine, a group of female leaders on the banks of the Putumayo are healing the wounds of conflict and transmitting their ancestral knowledge to new generations, demonstrating that resistance is also woven by hand.

DonationSection.subtitle: Join the Cause
DonationSection.title:
Your contribution builds
autonomy and dignity
DonationSection.p1: Cimarrón Putumayo is sustained by voluntary work and solidarity. With your donation, you directly support the training of youth, the protection of elders, cultural rescue, and productive projects in our communities.
DonationSection.p2: Together we can strengthen the Afro legacy in the Amazon.

NewsletterSection.title: Stay connected with our roots
NewsletterSection.p: Join our newsletter to receive stories, project news, open calls, and updates from Cimarrón Putumayo directly in your inbox.
NewsletterSection.msgSuccess: Thank you! You have successfully subscribed.

DefendemosSection.title: What do we defend?
DefendemosSection.p: We defend the right to life, territory, cultural identity, and equitable development. We work for a lasting peace where Afro-descendant communities are the protagonists of their own history in the Amazon.

1.title: Resistance
1.text: We stand firm in the defense of our rights and territory, overcoming obstacles with the strength of our ancestors.

2.title: Brotherhood
2.text: We build social fabric based on respect, mutual support, and community work (Uramba).

3.title: Identity
3.text: We celebrate and preserve our African roots through art, oral tradition, aesthetics, and spirituality.

4.title: Leadership
4.text: We train new generations with critical and proactive capacity to transform our reality.

HistoriaSection.title: Our History
HistoriaSection.p1: Cimarrón Putumayo is born from the desire of black communities to organize, be recognized, and defend their rights in a territory historically marked by state abandonment and armed conflict.
HistoriaSection.p2: Since our beginnings, we have worked tirelessly to make visible the Afro-descendant contribution to the culture and development of Putumayo. We started as a small collective and today we are a departmental network that articulates community councils and grassroots organizations.
HistoriaSection.p3: Over the years, we have led processes of training, cultural rescue, and rights enforceability, becoming a reference for peace, resistance, and dignity in the Amazon region.

1.title: HUMAN RIGHTS, PEACE AND TERRITORIAL PROTECTION
1.desc: We defend fundamental rights, life and territory as a sacred space of cultural and physical survival.

2.title: ETHNOEDUCATION, ART, CULTURE AND AFRO IDENTITY
2.desc: We rescue, preserve and disseminate our cultural heritage, ancestral knowledge and traditional practices.

3.title: WOMEN, GENDER AND LIVES FREE OF VIOLENCE
3.desc: We promote women's leadership and work for gender equity and a life free of all violence.

4.title: YOUTH, TRAINING AND GENERATIONAL RENEWAL
4.desc: We accompany children and youth in their comprehensive training and in building meaningful life projects.

5.title: SCHOOL OF LEADERSHIP AND ANCESTRAL RECOGNITION
5.desc: We train leadership with ethics, identity and community commitment for social transformation.

6.title: ENTREPRENEURSHIP AND ECONOMIC SUSTAINABILITY
6.desc: We promote economic and productive initiatives that strengthen autonomy and good living in the territory.

7.title: PEACE, RECONCILIATION AND SOCIAL FABRIC
7.desc: We build coexistence, reconciliation and social fabric for collective well-being and the defense of life.

MisionVisionSection.misionText: We are an organization committed to keeping alive the ancestral memory, cultural rescue, and the survival of our uses and customs, promoting the strengthening of children and youth through training, leadership, identity, and community participation processes, which boost their comprehensive development and active role in social transformation with dignity and autonomy.
MisionVisionSection.visionText: To be a benchmark Afro-Putumayan organization in Putumayo, Colombia, and internationally, recognized for its leadership in defending the rights of Afro-descendant communities, strengthening cultural identity, and promoting sustainable development and leadership processes with an ethnic, territorial, environmental, gender, and peace focus.

UneteCausaSection.title: Join Our Cause
UneteCausaSection.subtitle: Your support strengthens our communities<br class="hidden sm:block" /> and builds a future with dignity.
UneteCausaSection.info: Every contribution is a seed of hope for the Afro-descendant Putumayo.

ProjectsHero.title:
Projects that
sow dignity
ProjectsHero.p1: At Cimarrón Putumayo, we drive projects that strengthen cultural identity, promote human rights, open opportunities for women and youth, and build paths of peace, leadership, and autonomy for our communities.
ProjectsHero.p2: Every project is born from listening, from the living memory of our elders, from collective work, and from the conviction that development only makes sense when it respects the identity, history, and dignity of Afro-Putumayan peoples.

ProjectsCTA.title:
Let's work and
create community
projects
ProjectsCTA.p: The development of our territories depends on collective action. If you have an idea, initiative, or want to join efforts in our projects, the Cimarrón Putumayo Association has its doors open to articulate processes for the benefit of the communities.
ProjectsCTA.btn: I want to propose a project

ProjectsDirection.title: Project Management
ProjectsDirection.p: Our projects are led under the principles of transparency, community participation, and a differential approach. We ensure that each initiative has a real impact on the territory and respects the autonomy of our community councils.

FeaturedStory.title: The legacy of traditional singers in the Lower Putumayo
FeaturedStory.excerpt: The voices of our elder women resonate along the river, preserving melodies that crossed oceans. In this story, we explore how traditional chants have become a tool for community healing and peaceful resistance...

1.title: Guardians of the jungle: ancestral medicinal knowledge
1.excerpt: A journey through traditional gardens where ancestral medicine remains the first line of care.
1.author: Communications Committee

2.title: Uramba: The collective work that built the Afro House
2.excerpt: The story of how solidarity and teamwork made our main meeting point possible.
2.author: Cimarrón Association

3.title: Afro-Putumayan youth: new voices, same roots
3.excerpt: Young people are using urban art and digital media to tell the Afro story.
3.author: Youth Network

ContactForm.successText: Thank you for writing. We will contact you soon.
ContactForm.title: Send us a message
ContactForm.errName: Name must be at least 2 characters
ContactForm.errMessage: Message must be at least 10 characters

ContactPage.title: Contact
ContactPage.subtitle: We are here to listen to you. If you want to join our cause, have any questions or proposals, do not hesitate to write to us.
ContactPage.address2: Casa de Cultura Afroputumayense Librada Maturana

CasaAfroPage.desc1: A vital space for memory, gathering, and community building in the heart of Putumayo.
CasaAfroPage.desc2_1: The Casa Afro is more than a physical structure; it is the vibrant heart of our organization. Here our traditions intertwine, the future is planned, and life is celebrated.
CasaAfroPage.desc2_2: In this space, we hold workshops, community meetings, musical rehearsals, and cultural preservation activities.

uramba-cimarron.title: Uramba Cimarrón
uramba-cimarron.content: Uramba Cimarrón is a central initiative of our association aimed at strengthening the social fabric and community solidarity. Through collective work (Uramba), we seek to empower Afro-descendant communities in Putumayo, promoting community organization, the rescue of our ancestral traditions, and the construction of a more equitable and dignified future for all.

etnodesarrollo-afroputumayo.title: Ethnodevelopment Afroputumayo
etnodesarrollo-afroputumayo.content: This project focuses on promoting ethnodevelopment processes that strengthen economic autonomy and the territorial identity of our communities. We implement strategies that respect our worldview and the natural environment of the Amazon, encouraging sustainable agricultural practices and the defense of our territorial rights.

talento-afro-innovador.title: Innovative Afro Talent
talento-afro-innovador.content: With Innovative Afro Talent, we open doors for new Afro-Putumayan generations. This comprehensive training program offers tools in leadership, technology, entrepreneurship, and arts. Our goal is for young people to find safe spaces to develop their potential and become future leaders.

guardianes-rios.title: Guardians of the Amazon Rivers
guardianes-rios.content: Water is life and memory. Guardians of the Amazon Rivers is a project dedicated to the care of the territory, the protection of our rivers, and the preservation of ancestral environmental knowledge. We organize cleanup days and environmental education to ensure a healthy ecosystem.

formacion-derechos.title: Rights and Leadership Training
formacion-derechos.content: We firmly believe that education is the foundation of freedom. This project develops continuous pedagogical processes to strengthen active citizenship, gender equity, and political participation of the Afro-descendant population, empowering community leaders.

aulas-casa-cultura-2012.title: House of Culture Classrooms 2012
aulas-casa-cultura-2012.content:
Project: Construction of two classrooms of the Afroputumayan House of Culture

Funded by: Association of Spouses of Accredited Diplomats in Colombia.


Objective
To strengthen the organizational, cultural, educational, and community processes of the Afro-descendant population of the Putumayo department through the construction and adaptation of two classrooms in the Afroputumayan House of Culture.


Impacted Population
The project has directly and indirectly benefited more than 2,500 people belonging to Afro-descendant communities of the Putumayo department.


Results Achieved
The construction of the two classrooms allowed consolidating an adequate physical space for the development of educational, cultural, and organizational processes, contributing significantly to the institutional strengthening of the Afroputumayan House of Culture.

adecuacion-casa-cultura-2019.title: House of Culture Adaptation 2019
adecuacion-casa-cultura-2019.content:
Project: Adaptation of the Afroputumayan House of Culture

Cooperating entity: CISP


Objective
To adapt and strengthen the infrastructure of the Afroputumayan House of Culture as a safe, inclusive, and functional community space for the development of cultural, artistic, educational, and social integration activities.


Impacted Population
The project benefited directly and indirectly people belonging to black, Afro-descendant, indigenous, peasant communities, and other population groups of the Putumayo department.


Results
As a result of the adaptation of the Afroputumayan House of Culture, the security and functionality conditions of the space were significantly improved.

trabajo-cultural-continuo.title: Continuous Cultural Work
trabajo-cultural-continuo.content:
It constitutes a fundamental tool for the preservation, strengthening, and transmission of the identity of the Afroputumayan communities.


In accordance with the principles of recognition, respect for ethnic and cultural diversity, autonomy, community participation, and strengthening of collective identity, permanent cultural work allows children, youth, adults, and elders to know, value, and appropriate our history.


Likewise, the continuous development of cultural processes contributes to the protection of tangible and intangible heritage.


Cultural work also represents a strategy of resistance against the dynamics of racial discrimination, social exclusion, and loss of historical memory.

ProjectProposalModal.title: Propose a Project
ProjectProposalModal.description: Tell us your idea or initiative. We are ready to listen and join efforts.
ProjectProposalModal.successMsg: Thank you! We have received your proposal. We will contact you soon.

ProjectsHistory.desc: A journey through the actions and processes that have strengthened our community, our culture, and our organizational path.
